title: Liberalism vs conservatism

audience_match: Members of the Philippine bench and bar
Scholars of comparative religious and constitutional liberalization

signature_phrases.phrase: Our Supreme Court and the Catholic Churchâ€”the bulwarks of conservatism and traditionâ€”are moving progressively on a crucial but rocky road to liberalism
the Supreme Court of the United States (Scotus), the traditional bastion of liberalism, has turned conservative
US citizens will face a crossroad
AN EXAMPLE OF THE COURT'S TREK TO LIBERALISM
Philippine courts can recognize divorces obtained abroad by a Filipino married to a foreigner
a statute cannot always be interpreted literally; rather it 'must be read according to its spirit or intent'
Courts ought not to â€¦ accept an interpretation that would defeat the intent of the law and its legislators
the legislative spirit animating Article 26(2) of the Family Code
it would be unjust for a Filipino spouse to be prohibited by [his/her] national laws from something that a foreign law may allow
our laws should not be intended to put Filipinos at a disadvantage
Another example: Pulido v. People
an accused may be acquitted of bigamy 'even without obtaining a judicial declaration of absolute nullity'
Frankly, I am surprised at the liberal path of the Court
13 members are 'insiders' who, having risen from the judicial ranks, are expected to be conservative
Only two members are 'outsiders'â€”from the University of the Philippines College of Law and the Department of Justice, who, to no surprise, are liberals
THE CATHOLIC CHURCH HAS ALSO BEEN LIBERALIZING
When I was a student, priests faced the altar during masses, mumbling unintelligible (to the faithful) Latin that frankly turned me off
Vatican II (1962-1965) has made the Church more humane and ecumenical
Masses are now celebrated by priests facing the faithful and in languages understood by them
Pope (now Saint) John Paul II slowlyâ€”but with grit and determination
Pope Francis, the first non-European pontiff
All religions are paths to reach God
They are like different languages in order to arrive at God, but God is God for all
These extemporaneous remarks will surely rock the rafters of the Catholic Church
IN CONTRAST, THE LIBERALISM OF THE SCOTUS has forked to conservatism
Neil Gorsuch, Brett Kavanaugh, and Amy Coney Barrett
upsetting the usual 5-4 Scotus balance to 6-3 in favor of the conservatives
Roe v. Wade
Dobbs v. Jackson
an acrimonious combat between liberals and conservatives
the liberal-conservative divide will shake the whole world
My fervent hope is that liberalism would not transform into licentiousness for that would subvert the rule of law
conservatism would not convert to authoritarianism like Hitler's Nazism for that would violate individual liberty
I am terrified by the rants of Trump that, if elected, he would become a dictator
Heaven forbid!

stances.claim: The Philippine Supreme Court and the Catholic Church â€” traditional bulwarks of conservatism â€” are moving progressively along a rocky road to liberalism, while SCOTUS, the traditional bastion of liberalism, has turned conservative.

Republic v Ng (Feb 27, 2024, J Dimaampao, en banc, 13-2) recognized that Philippine courts can give effect to divorces obtained abroad by Filipinos married to foreigners through legal, administrative, or mutual-agreement processes â€” not requiring a foreign court judgment â€” by reading the statute according to its spirit and intent, which is to spare Filipino spouses from disadvantage when the foreign spouse is already free to remarry.

Pulido v People (July 27, 2021, J Hernando, en banc, 15-0) further illustrates the liberal trek by holding that an accused may be acquitted of bigamy even without a judicial declaration of absolute nullity of a void-ab-initio first marriage â€” though judicial declaration is still needed for remarriage and for civil-rights determinations like heirship, legitimacy of children, and property regimes.

CJ is surprised by the SC's liberal path because 13 of its 15 members are 'insiders' who rose from the judicial ranks and would be expected to be conservative; only two outsiders (UP and DOJ) are predictably liberal.

The Catholic Church has been liberalizing through Vatican II (1962-1965) â€” vernacular masses, priests facing the faithful â€” implemented in the Philippines via PCP2; Pope John Paul II carried it forward slowly but determinedly; Pope Francis as the first non-European pontiff is even more determined, and his Sept 13 Singapore extemporaneous remarks ('All religions are paths to reach Godâ€¦ God is God for all') 'will surely rock the rafters' and intensify traditionalist opposition.

SCOTUS, by contrast, was tipped from 5-4 to 6-3 conservative by Trump's three appointees (Gorsuch, Kavanaugh, Barrett); Dobbs v Jackson (June 24, 2022) reversed Roe v Wade (Jan 22, 1973) and held no constitutional right to abortion exists in the US Constitution's text.

Together with immigration, democracy, jobs, and the economy, the conservative SCOTUS shift on abortion has turned the Nov 5 Harris-Trump contest into 'acrimonious combat between liberals and conservatives' whose ramifications will shake the wars in Ukraine, Gaza, and Lebanon, the tensions in the North and South China Sea, and the Israel-Iran standoff.

CJ's fervent hope is that liberalism not become licentiousness subverting the rule of law, and that conservatism not become Hitler-style Nazism violating individual liberty â€” but he is 'terrified' by Trump's rants that, if elected, 'he would become a dictator.' 'Heaven forbid!'

notable_anecdotes.summary: The title-as-thesis inversion â€” 'Our Supreme Court and the Catholic Churchâ€¦ are moving progressively on a crucial but rocky road to liberalism. In contrast, the Supreme Court of the United Statesâ€¦ has turned conservative.' â€” a paragraph that distills the entire column's structural argument.

CJ's autobiographical 'When I was a student, priests faced the altar during masses, mumbling unintelligible (to the faithful) Latin that frankly turned me off' â€” a rare personal admission of pre-Vatican-II alienation as the experiential warrant for the liberalization narrative.

Pope Francis's Singapore quote rendered verbatim â€” 'All religions are paths to reach Godâ€¦ They are like different languages in order to arrive at God, but God is God for allâ€¦ Since God is God for all, then we are all children of God' â€” with explicit prediction that the remarks 'will surely rock the rafters.'

The 13-insider vs 2-outsider SC compositional observation â€” CJ's surprise that judicial-rank insiders nonetheless trended liberal, contradicting the easy career-tenure-equals-conservatism heuristic.

The Trump-as-dictator fear-disclosure â€” 'I am terrified by the rants of Trump that, if elected, he would become a dictator. Heaven forbid!' â€” one of the most personally direct political-warning closes in this set.

The Nazism-as-warning frame â€” invoking Hitler explicitly as the conservatism-gone-wrong endpoint, paired with licentiousness as the liberalism-gone-wrong endpoint, modeling CJ's twin-warning approach.
The Sept 13 dating of Francis's Singapore remarks at the end of a 12-day journey through Indonesia, Papua New Guinea, Timor-Leste â€” concrete itinerary as context, not just headline.

one_paragraph_summary: Inverting the usual stereotype, Panganiban argues that the Philippine Supreme Court and the Catholic Church â€” traditional bulwarks of conservatism â€” are moving progressively on a 'rocky road to liberalism,' while the US Supreme Court (Scotus), traditional bastion of liberalism, has turned conservative. The PH SC's liberal trek is illustrated by Republic v Ng (Feb 27, 2024, J Dimaampao, en banc, 13-2), which recognized foreign divorces â€” even by administrative process or mutual agreement (as practiced in Japan and Korea) â€” by reading Article 26(2) of the Family Code according to its spirit and intent (sparing Filipino spouses from disadvantage when foreign spouses are already free to remarry), and by Pulido v People (July 27, 2021, J Hernando, en banc, 15-0), which acquitted an accused of bigamy even without a judicial declaration of nullity of a void-ab-initio first marriage (though judicial declaration is still needed for remarriage and civil-rights questions like heirship). CJ is surprised that 13 insider-justices trended liberal, with only two outsiders (UP, DOJ) predictably liberal. The Catholic Church has likewise been liberalizing â€” Vatican II (1962-1965) made it more humane and ecumenical (vernacular masses, priests facing the faithful), PCP2 implemented this in the Philippines, Pope John Paul II carried it forward, and Pope Francis as the first non-European pontiff is even more determined; his Sept 13 Singapore extemporaneous remarks ('All religions are paths to reach Godâ€¦ God is God for all, then we are all children of God') will 'rock the rafters' and intensify traditionalist opposition. By contrast, Scotus tipped from 5-4 to 6-3 conservative with Trump's three appointees (Gorsuch, Kavanaugh, Barrett), and Dobbs v Jackson (June 24, 2022) reversed Roe v Wade (Jan 22, 1973). Combined with immigration, democracy, jobs, and the economy, abortion has turned the Nov 5 Harris-Trump contest into 'acrimonious combat between liberals and conservatives' whose ramifications will shake the wars in Ukraine, Gaza, and Lebanon, the tensions in the North and South China Sea, and the Israel-Iran standoff. CJ's fervent hope is that liberalism not become 'licentiousness' subverting the rule of law, and that conservatism not become Hitler-style Nazism violating individual liberty â€” but he is 'terrified by the rants of Trump that, if elected, he would become a dictator. Heaven forbid!'
